vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF for #725: scripts/release_rollback.py uses an unsafe `git reset --hard HEAD~1` (unwinds whatever happens to be at HEAD when commits land between release-prep and rollback) AND `git push --force-with-lease origin master` (rejected by GitHub branch protection rules disallowing force-push). Replace with a resolved-SHA forward `git revert` flow and a normal (non-force) push so rollback works end-to-end on a protected default branch.

plan.title: Replace HEAD~1 reset + force-push with resolved-SHA forward-revert + normal push in scripts/release_rollback.py

narratives.Problem: scripts/release_rollback.py::git_reset_head_minus_one blindly resets to HEAD~1, assuming the release-prep commit is the latest commit. That assumption breaks the moment ANY commit lands between release-prep and the rollback invocation -- which is a normal operational scenario (fix a release defect via PR, then decide to rollback). Live demonstration: PR #722 merged on top of release-prep commit 6573335; `task release:rollback -- v0.21.0 --allow-data-loss` then reset master from 94d1aa5 -> 6573335, unwinding PR #722 instead of release-prep. Defect B coupled to A: scripts/release_rollback.py::git_force_push_base calls `git push --force-with-lease origin master`, which is rejected by GitHub branch-protection rules disallowing force-push (default config for protected master). The script aborts after gh release delete already succeeded, leaving master in a broken local-only state.
narratives.Overview: Resolve the actual release-prep commit SHA at runtime (preferred: `git rev-parse v<version>^{commit}` BEFORE the tag is deleted; fallback: `git log --grep='^chore(release): v<version>' -n 1 --format=%H` walking back from HEAD). Replace `git_reset_head_minus_one` with `git_revert_release_commit(project_root, sha)` that runs `git revert <sha> --no-edit`. On conflict the helper aborts the revert and returns a clear refusal pointing the operator at the manual-recovery hint in the script docstring. Replace `git_force_push_base` with `git_push_base` that runs a normal `git push origin <base-branch>` (no force, branch-protection-compatible). State 1 (local-only) drops the remote push entirely -- nothing was pushed so there is nothing to push back. States 2 and 3 push the new revert commit forward.
narratives.Background: Surfaced during the v0.21.0 rollback-as-test on 2026-04-28 (#721). The state-aware unwind (#716) works correctly through state detection, guard logic, and gh release delete --cleanup-tag, but the commit-unwind + push phase has these two coupled defects. Defect A's fix (forward revert) makes Defect B's force-push moot because we no longer rewrite history; Defect B's branch-protection refusal would have surfaced even with the correct SHA in Defect A's reset path, so they MUST be fixed together. The companion #722 (subprocess PATHEXT fix) is already merged and exposes `release._resolve_gh()` -- this PR re-uses `release._run_git()` for git invocations.
narratives.Constraint: MUST preserve existing exit codes (0 success / 1 violation / 2 config error), the four-state machine (local-only / tag-pushed-no-release / released / absent), the time-windowed download-count guard, and the race-condition double-read. MUST NOT widen scope beyond the rollback unwind + push phase. MUST keep the public CLI surface stable (no new flags). MUST update the module docstring's state table so the operator-readable contract matches the new behaviour.
narratives.Risk: Low. The revert flow is strictly safer than reset --hard (it preserves history and is auditable), and a forward push to a protected branch is the documented path. The conflict-refusal path defends against the rare case where a commit on top of release-prep touched a file the release-prep commit also touched (CHANGELOG.md / ROADMAP.md) -- the operator gets a manual-recovery hint instead of a half-unwound state.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) task release:rollback -- v<version> works end-to-end against a master branch with branch protection enabled (no force-push required); (2) regression test in tests/cli/test_release_rollback.py simulates a commit landing between release-prep and rollback, asserts the unwind targets the release-prep commit (not HEAD~1); (3) regression test asserts `git push origin <base-branch>` is invoked WITHOUT --force / --force-with-lease; (4) regression test asserts the conflict path refuses cleanly with a manual-recovery hint; (5) module docstring's state table updated to reflect the new git revert + git push (no force) flow.
narratives.Test: Verification: tests/cli/test_release_rollback.py covers (a) resolve_release_prep_sha returns the rev-parse result when the tag exists; (b) resolve_release_prep_sha falls back to `git log --grep` when rev-parse fails; (c) resolve_release_prep_sha returns ('', reason) when both probes fail; (d) git_revert_release_commit invokes `git revert <sha> --no-edit`; (e) git_revert_release_commit detects conflict (non-zero rc + recognised stderr) and runs `git revert --abort`, returning False with a manual-recovery hint; (f) git_push_base invokes `git push origin <base-branch>` with NO --force / --force-with-lease; (g) _unwind_local / _unwind_tag_pushed_no_release / _unwind_released call resolve_release_prep_sha BEFORE deleting the tag, AND pass the resolved SHA (not HEAD~1) to the revert helper; (h) the new push helper is the only push path (no force-push remains anywhere). Existing rollback tests continue to pass.
narratives.Action: Swarm-scope leaf: independent of any other vBRIEF (touches only scripts/release_rollback.py + tests/cli/test_release_rollback.py + CHANGELOG); gated only on master. Closes #725 once merged.

items.title: Add resolve_release_prep_sha helper to scripts/release_rollback.py with rev-parse + grep fallback

narrative.Action: New `def resolve_release_prep_sha(project_root: Path, version: str) -> tuple[str, str]` returning (sha, reason). Try `git rev-parse v<version>^{commit}` first (works whenever the tag still resolves locally OR a tag-shaped probe walks the repo); on failure fall back to `git log --grep='^chore(release): v<version>' --format=%H -n 1`. Return ('', diagnostic) when neither resolves so callers can refuse cleanly.
narrative.Acceptance: Unit tests cover the rev-parse happy path, grep fallback, and the all-probes-failed refusal.

items.title: Replace git_reset_head_minus_one + git_force_push_base with git_revert_release_commit + git_push_base across all unwind branches

narrative.Action: Add `git_revert_release_commit(project_root, sha)` running `git revert <sha> --no-edit`; on non-zero rc run `git revert --abort` and return (False, manual-recovery hint). Add `git_push_base(project_root, base_branch)` running `git push origin <base>` (NO --force / --force-with-lease). Update `_unwind_local` / `_unwind_tag_pushed_no_release` / `_unwind_released` to call `resolve_release_prep_sha` BEFORE deleting the tag (release_e2e cleanup-tag deletes both refs), and pass the resolved SHA into the revert helper. State 1 (local-only) drops the push entirely (nothing was pushed). States 2 and 3 push the new revert commit forward via `git_push_base`.
narrative.Acceptance: Tests assert intervening-commit safety (resolved SHA is the revert target, NOT HEAD~1), no-force push semantics (argv contains 'push' + 'origin' + base-branch, NO '--force' / '--force-with-lease'), and clean conflict refusal (revert --abort runs, return value carries the manual-recovery hint).

items.title: Update scripts/release_rollback.py module docstring state table to git revert + git push (no force); add CHANGELOG entry

narrative.Action: Replace the state-table prose `git reset --hard HEAD~1` and `git push --force-with-lease` cells with `git revert <release-prep-sha> --no-edit` and `git push origin <base>` (no force). Add a Manual Recovery section to the docstring describing the steps the operator runs when revert conflicts. CHANGELOG entry: '[Unreleased]/Fixed: Replace unsafe HEAD~1 reset + force-push with resolved-SHA forward revert + normal push in scripts/release_rollback.py so the rollback flow is safe across intervening commits and compatible with GitHub branch protection (#725).'
narrative.Acceptance: Docstring rendering shows the new state-table cells; CHANGELOG.md `## [Unreleased] / ### Fixed` contains the entry.

x-tracking.scope_authority: deft-725-fix sub-agent dispatch (swarm Agent 1; parent monitor's plan stages 1-4)

references.title: Issue #725: rollback script uses unsafe HEAD~1 reset + branch-protection-incompatible force-push
Issue #716: release safety hardening (canonical spec; rollback semantics origin)
Issue #722: subprocess PATHEXT fix (introduces release._resolve_gh helper re-used here)
Issue #721: rollback-as-test recovery anchor (full session transcript surfacing #725)
